title: Apply timestamped key-value patches
spec: Input is JSON Lines. Each line is an object with fields `ts` (integer), `op` (`set` or `delete`), `key` (string), and optionally `value` (string). Apply operations in ascending `ts`; when timestamps tie, preserve input order among tied records. `set` stores the value for the key. `delete` removes the key if present. Output the final map as lines `key=value`, sorted by key in ascending bytewise/lexicographic order. If the final map is empty, print nothing.
